data.title: Euro Area trade deficit widens in January
data.description:
• Euro Area recorded a trade deficit of Є1.9B in January of 2026, compared with - €1.4B in January 2025.
  • Exports of goods to the rest of the world in January were €215.3B, a decrease of 7.6% compared with January 2025.
  • Imports from the rest of the world stood at €217.2B, a fall of 7.3% compared with January last year.
  • ETFs to track: (GF), (EWG), (DAX), (EWI), (EWQ), (FGM).

data.title: Amazon developing Alexa-linked phone to deepen customer reach, Reuters reports
data.description:
• In 2014, Amazon (AMZN) launched its Fire Phone to rival Apple (AAPL) and Samsung (SSNLF), but the device - overseen by Jeff Bezos - was scrapped within a year; now, the company is preparing to try again with a new smartphone, Reuters reported on Friday.
  • The latest effort, known internally as “Transformer,” is being developed within its devices and services unit, according to four ​people familiar with the matter. The phone is considered a potential mobile personalization device that can sync with home voice assistant Alexa and serve as a conduit to Amazon customers throughout the day, the report said.
  • The timeline for the project is unclear, as well as the anticipated price ​of the phone, the revenue Amazon (AMZN) hopes to generate, or the financial commitment Amazon has made to the project
  • Amazon spokesperson declined to comment to Reuters.

data.title: AM Markets Need to Know: Oil prices slip, ABC cancels The Bachelorette, and more
data.description:
Stock index futures were firmly in the green on Friday as market sentiment improved after Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu said the country is helping to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

Now, here are 5 news stories that broke overnight to watch out for:

Crude oil futures ease: U.S. crude oil futures eased after-hours Thursday after Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu said Iran no longer has the capacity to enrich uranium or make ballistic missiles and that Israel will help reopen the Strait of Hormuz. “We are winning, and Iran is being decimated,” Netanyahu told a news conference, noting that Iran’s missile and drone arsenal is being massively degraded.

Russian oil restrictions expanded: The U.S. Treasury Department has added Cuba to a list of countries restricted from receiving Russian oil after a tanker carrying the fuel was reported to be heading to the island, which is already under longstanding U.S. sanctions. In an updated general license issued Thursday, Treasury expanded the list of countries barred from transactions involving the sale, delivery, or offloading of Russian crude and petroleum products. The license also applies to Iran, North Korea, and Russian-occupied regions of Ukraine.

JPMorgan tracks junior bankers: JPMorgan Chase (JPM) will issue reports to junior bankers that compare computer-generated estimates of their workweek against self-reported timesheets as part of a pilot scheme, the Financial Times reported. The estimate is based on employees’ weekly digital footprint, including video calls, desktop keystrokes, and scheduled meetings. The bank plans to roll out the program more widely across its investment bank.

ABC cancels “The Bachelorette”: Walt Disney (DIS)-owned ABC could lose tens of millions if it fails to salvage “The Bachelorette,” sources told Variety, with potential losses including Warner Bros. (WBD) unscripted TV license fees, marketing expenses, and other revenue streams. With the release of a 2023 video showing star Taylor Frankie Paul throwing chairs at her estranged partner Dakota Mortensen—and accidentally hitting her child—ABC abruptly pulled the show on Thursday, after having sworn throughout the week that it had no plans to do so.

IEA issues oil demand-side actions: The International Energy Agency has listed 10 demand-side actions that governments, businesses, and households can implement to shield themselves from the oil shock. These include working from home, avoiding air travel, reducing speed limits on highways by at least 6 miles an hour, carpooling, and switching to electric cooking. The IEA said the actions focus primarily on road transport, which accounts for around 45% of global oil demand.

data.title: Work from home, fly less - IEA pitches demand-side actions to offset oil shock
data.description:
The International Energy Agency has listed 10 demand-side actions that governments, businesses and households can implement to shield themselves from the oil shock. 

These include working from home, avoiding air travel, reducing speed limits on highways by at least ~6 miles an hour, carpooling and switching to electric cooking.

The IEA said the actions focus primarily on road transport, which accounts for around 45% of global oil demand, but also cover aviation, cooking and industry. 

"The war in the Middle East is creating a major energy crisis, including the largest supply disruption in the history of the global oil market," said IEA Executive Director Fatih Birol. "In the absence of a swift resolution, the impacts on energy markets and economies are set to become more and more severe."

The agency said restoring transit through the Strait of Hormuz remains essential to stabilize global energy markets.

"In the meantime, countries are acting on both supply and demand," it noted, pointing to IEA member countries' decision to release 400M barrels of oil from emergency reserves.

As supply-side measures alone can't mitigate the disruption, "addressing demand is a critical and immediate tool to reduce pressure on consumers by improving affordability and supporting energy security," the IEA said.

data.title: Cheniere, Venture Global rise as Iran strikes knock out Qatar LNG capacity
data.description:
Shares of top U.S. liquefied natural gas producers Cheniere Energy (LNG) and Venture Global (VG) climbed about 3% on Friday after Iran’s attacks on Qatar’s LNG infrastructure knocked out a significant portion of its production, tightening global energy supplies and boosting demand for U.S. exporters.

Cheniere Energy, the largest exporter of LNG in the United States, was trading 3% higher at $290.42, after hitting an all-time high in the previous session. Venture Global (VG), the second-ranked U.S. producer meanwhile also advanced 3% after QatarEnergy's comments. 

Iranian attacks ‌have knocked out 17% of Qatar's liquefied natural gas (LNG) export capacity, causing an estimated $20 billion in lost annual revenue and threatening supplies to Europe and Asia, QatarEnergy's CEO and state minister for energy affairs told Reuters on Thursday.

Saad al-Kaabi said two of Qatar's 14 LNG trains and one of its two gas-to-liquids (GTL) facilities were damaged in ​the unprecedented strikes. The repairs will sideline 12.8 million tons per year of LNG for three to five years, he said ​in an interview.

Cheniere can export more than 51 million metric tons of ​LNG per year, while Venture Global can ship out more than 37 million tons, according to the companies’ recent earnings calls.

The conflict that erupted late last month has rattled global energy markets, closing the Strait of Hormuz, halting roughly a fifth of worldwide oil flows, and forcing QatarEnergy to halt LNG shipments, with analysts now warning of longer-term impacts on gas and LNG prices.

According to IWA Research, Cheniere's long-term contracts, recent CPC agreement, and ongoing expansion enhance predictability and secure future growth amid global volatility. The stock has risen 45% year-to-date, while Venture Global has surged 109% over the same period.

SA investing group leader Value Portfolio said attacks on Qatar and the Strait of Hormuz closure boosted Venture Global, which still offers a valuable opportunity despite higher prices. "The company has a staggering 68 MTPA of LNG production in operation or under construction, which positions it to be one of the largest LNG producers in the world."

Since the U.S. and Israel started bombing Iran on February 28, U.S. gas prices have ​jumped about 12% compared to gains of 91% in Europe and 88% in Asia, Reuters reported. Gas is trading at 37-month highs near $21 per million British thermal units (mmBtu) at the Dutch ‌Title Transfer ⁠Facility (TTF) benchmark in Europe and near $20 at the Japan-Korea Marker (JKM) benchmark in Asia.

data.title: Torrid outlines $940M–$960M net sales outlook for 2026 while accelerating customer growth initiatives
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Torrid Holdings Inc. (CURV) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Lisa Harper highlighted that Torrid reached the top end of its net sales outlook for 2025 with $1 billion and exceeded the high end of its adjusted EBITDA range at $63.6 million, stating, "We have optimized our channel, product and pricing platforms. With these foundational elements now in place, I'll outline our primary focus for 2026, which is accelerating customer file growth through retention, reactivation and acquisition."
  • Harper noted deliberate decisions throughout the year, such as optimizing the store footprint, launching the sub-brand strategy, pausing and relaunching the footwear category, and sharpening product assortment around core franchises, fabrications, and silhouettes.
  • The CEO reported strong category trends, including four consecutive quarters of growth in dresses, acceleration in sub-brands, a turnaround in knit tops, and momentum in Intimates and Activewear. Footwear was reintroduced with a successful sell-out of a limited assortment, with plans to scale the category profitably in the back half of 2026.
  • Sub-brands generated over $70 million in sales in 2025, with management projecting approximately 60% growth to $110 million in 2026, aiming to move from 7% of total net sales in 2025 to 12% in 2026.
  • Harper emphasized that opening price point (OPP) strategy now represents about 30% of the assortment and nearly 40% in stores, stating, "We are seeing the most loved opening price point collection drive conversion and UPT in both channels, and we believe that this will be a critical component of customer file growth."
  • CFO Paula Dempsey stated, "Fiscal 2025 was a year of intentional structural change. We delivered full year sales of $1 billion in line with our guidance and an adjusted EBITDA of $63.6 million, slightly ahead of expectations. Most importantly, we achieved as well simultaneously executing a significant transformation of our physical footprint, proactively managing an estimated $50 million in gross tariff headwinds and maintaining the inventory discipline that leaves us entering fiscal 2026 in a balanced inventory position."

Outlook

  • Dempsey reported, "For the full year, we expect net sales of $940 million to $960 million and adjusted EBITDA of $65 million to $75 million, representing margin expansion up to 140 basis points off of fiscal 2025."
  • For Q1 2026, guidance is for sales of $236 million to $244 million and adjusted EBITDA of $14 million to $18 million.
  • Capital expenditures for the year are expected in the range of $8 million to $10 million, reflecting continued reinvestment discipline.
  • Management stated that the store optimization program will be substantially complete by mid-2026, expecting to close up to an additional 30 stores by then.

Financial Results

  • Q4 net sales were $236.2 million. Comparable sales declined 10%, including a 460 basis point negative comp impact due to the temporary pause of the shoe business.
  • Gross profit for Q4 was $70.9 million, and gross margin was 30% compared to 33.6% in the prior year, reflecting promotional activity, product mix, and deleverage on a reduced sales base.
  • SG&A expenses declined by $11.4 million to $62.4 million. As a percentage of net sales, SG&A leveraged 40 basis points to 26.4%.
  • Marketing investment decreased to $13.5 million. Q4 net loss was $8.1 million, or $0.08 per share. Adjusted EBITDA was $5.2 million, a 2.2% margin.
  • Inventory at year end was $136.5 million, down 8%, and total liquidity was $84.9 million.

Q&A

  • Janine Hoffman Stichter, BTIG: Asked about sub-brand performance and growth expectations; Harper responded that Festi, Nightfall, and Retro are particularly strong performers, with Festi being the #1 sub-brand, and indicated that sub-brands are projected to reach about $110 million in 2026. Stichter also inquired about lapsed customer retention; Ashlee Wheeler explained that economic pressure on price was a primary reason for lapsing, which the OPP strategy addresses.
  • Dana Telsey, Telsey Advisory Group: Asked about holiday sales cadence, tariffs, and footwear return. Harper said tariff pressures persist but have been managed, and footwear will be a headwind in H1 2026 but a benefit in H2. Wheeler noted holiday performed as expected with improvement in January.
  • Brooke Roach, Goldman Sachs: Asked about marketing, pricing, and loyalty. Harper said promo strategy is shifting toward everyday value and price parity, with less reliance on Torrid cash redemption, and loyalty will focus on increasing frequency. Wheeler highlighted leveraging owned channels and direct mail. Roach also asked about EBITDA margins; Dempsey stated, "I do believe so. And I will tell you that our plan, we have up to 150 basis points of EBITDA margin increase in fiscal '26."
  • Corey Tarlowe, Jefferies: Asked about the shift from a defensive to an offensive strategy; Harper stated the company is now positioned to "start reaping the benefits" of recent structural changes. On pricing and promotions, Harper described a targeted promotional approach to reactivate and build frequency.
  • Dylan Carden, William Blair: Inquired about consumer behavior changes and refund impact. Wheeler reported consistent demographic performance, and Harper said no outsized refund impact is embedded in guidance.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed on sub-brand growth, customer retention strategies, and execution of promotional and pricing strategies, displaying a slightly positive to neutral tone with some cautious optimism and focus on execution risks.
  • Management maintained a confident and detailed tone in prepared remarks, with Harper stating, "This is our #1 priority, and we are deploying resources, talent and capital accordingly." During Q&A, management responses were thorough, direct, and showed increased confidence compared to prior quarters.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both management and analysts displayed increased optimism, with less focus on merchandising missteps and more on strategic execution and growth initiatives.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter shows a shift from the defensive stance of Q3, which focused on addressing assortment missteps, to a more offensive strategy emphasizing customer growth, sub-brand expansion, and store optimization completion.
  • Sub-brand sales targets for 2026 are now more conservative, with management aiming for mid-teens as a percentage of sales, compared to previous discussions of mid-20s.
  • Analysts in Q4 focused more on growth levers and marketing strategies than on past execution issues. Management demonstrated higher confidence, emphasizing foundational improvements and readiness for scalable growth, contrasting with the caution and corrective tone in Q3.
  • Key financial metrics show improved adjusted EBITDA and cost controls, with structural changes expected to yield further margin expansion in 2026.

Risks and Concerns

  • Ongoing tariff pressures remain a headwind, particularly in the first half of 2026, especially for footwear.
  • Management identified economic pressure on customers as a factor in lapsed retention but pointed to OPP strategy as mitigation.
  • Footwear absence contributed to negative comps, with the impact expected to reverse in H2 2026.
  • Store closures and associated retention risks are being actively managed, with customer migration to digital and nearby locations exceeding expectations.

Final Takeaway

Torrid Holdings enters 2026 with foundational changes in place across channel, product, and pricing platforms. The company is focused on accelerating customer file growth through targeted marketing, sub-brand expansion, and a disciplined approach to cost management. With guidance for $940 million to $960 million in net sales and adjusted EBITDA of $65 million to $75 million, management conveys confidence in margin expansion and scalability, driven by the completion of store optimization, reinvigorated product platforms, and operational discipline.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Biggest stock movers Friday: FDX, SMCI, TGNA and more
data.description:
U.S. stock futures edged higher amid volatile trading on Friday, while oil prices eased as investors assessed efforts by the U.S. and Israel to calm concerns over the conflict in Iran.

Here are some of Friday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • FedEx (FDX) +11% - Stock gained in premarket trading after the company topped expectations with its fiscal third-quarter earnings report. The Memphis-based shipping giant reported revenue was up 8.1% year-over-year to $24.0B in FQ3. EPS came in at $5.25 for the quarter that ended on February 28 vs. $4.15 consensus and $4.51 a year ago. The company said Federal Express segment operating results improved during the quarter, driven by higher U.S. domestic and international priority package yields, continued cost savings from transformation initiatives, and increased U.S. domestic package volume. 
  • Tegna (TGNA) +9% - Shares advanced as the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) on Thursday approved the $6.2B sale of Tegna (TGNA) to Nexstar (NXST), moving forward despite mounting opposition, including lawsuits from DirecTV and eight states, including California, seeking to block the deal on antitrust grounds. Nexstar Media (NXST) soon announced it had closed its acquisition of Tegna following approval from the FCC and U.S. Justice Department.
  • Cheniere Energy (LNG) +3% - Shares extended gains after rising to an all-time high in the previous session, while ​Venture Global (VG) also rose after QatarEnergy said attacks by Iran may shut nearly a fifth of its ‌LNG output for up to five years. The rally followed comments from QatarEnergy CEO Saad al‑Kaabi, who told Reuters ​that Iranian strikes had knocked out 17% of the Gulf nation’s LNG export capacity. Al‑Kaabi said two of Qatar’s 14 LNG trains and one ​of its two gas‑to‑liquids plants were damaged. 
  • Orla Mining (ORLA) +3% Shares rose after a quarterly beat. Fourth quarter gold production was 95,405 ounces and gold sold was 92,889 ounces. Total annual gold production for 2025 was 300,620 ounces and the company exceeded its full-year 2025 production guidance.

Biggest stock losers

  • Super Micro Computer (SMCI) -20% - Shares fell after three individuals linked to the AI server maker, including a co-founder, were charged with assisting in the smuggling of at least $2.5 billion worth of U.S. AI technology to China, in violation of export laws, the U.S. Justice Department said Thursday. U.S. prosecutors did not name Super ‌Micro in the complaint, referring only to a "U.S. manufacturer." San Jose, California-based Super Micro said it was informed by federal prosecutors of the indictment on Thursday. It noted that the company itself was not named as a defendant in the case and said it had cooperated with investigators.

data.title: Nvidia to sell 1M AI chips, other products to AWS by end of 2027: report
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) will sell 1M AI chips and other ​offerings to Amazon's (AMZN) Amazon Web Services by 2027, Reuters reported.

During Nvidia's annual GTC event this week, AWS said on March 16 that starting this year, it will add more than 1M Nvidia graphics processing units, or GPUs, including Blackwell and Rubin GPU architectures, across its global cloud regions. The two are also collaborating on Spectrum networking and other infrastructure areas.

Ian Buck, vice president of hyperscale and high-performance computing ​at Nvidia, told the news agency on Thursday that the sales would start ⁠this year and extend through 2027.

Among the major announcements by Nvidia's (NVDA) Founder and CEO Jensen Huang at GTC was the $1T revenue opportunity from Blackwell and Rubin chip demand through 2027.

Nvidia and Amazon did not disclose the financial terms of their deal. But Buck said the transaction contains a broad mix of Nvidia ​chips beyond the 1M GPUs, including Nvidia's Spectrum networking chips and ​the Groq chips that Nvidia released this week, the report added.

Nvidia and Amazon did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha on the additional product sales and the timeline.

In particular, AWS intends to use a combination of Nvidia's Groq chips and six others from Nvidia for more efficient inference.

AI inference is the process where a trained AI model applies its learned patterns to new data to make predictions, generate content, or make decisions.

"Inference is hard. ⁠It's wickedly hard," said Buck. "To be the best at inference, it is not a one chip pony. We actually ​use all seven chips."

The transaction also includes putting Nvidia's Connect ​X and ⁠Spectrum X networking products in AWS data centers. The move is important as AWS data centers use custom networking equipment that AWS has spent years perfecting, the report noted.

"They're still ⁠going to ​do that, of course," said Buck. "But we are ​collaborating now on deploying Connect X and Spectrum X for those important workloads and biggest customers ​across AI with AWS," the report added.

data.title: Meta backtracks on decision to drop Horizon Worlds from VR headsets
data.description:
Meta (META) has backtracked on its decision to shut down the Horizon Worlds platform for its Quest virtual reality headsets after users pushed back on the move.

"We have decided that we will keep Horizon Worlds working in VR for existing games to support the fans who've reached out," Meta's (META) CTO Andrew Bosworth said in an Instagram story.

He said the Horizon Unity game engine will only be available in VR and no new games will be added. "Most of our energy is going towards mobile and the Meta Horizon Engine because that's where most of the consumer and creator energy already was."

"For people who already have games they like that they're using in Horizon Worlds, you'll be able to download the Horizon Worlds app and use it in VR for the foreseeable future," Bosworth added.

Meta (META) has been shifting investments away from its metaverse efforts and recently laid off 10% of employees in its VR unit Reality Labs.

Horizon Worlds, once a key part of the company's VR push, had about 200,000-300,000 monthly active users at its peak in 2022, but that number has kept declining since then. In contrast, Roblox (RBLX) has over 150M daily active users.

data.title: European indexes rebound after oil prices ease
data.description:
London (UKX) +0.50% to 10,113. UK public sector net borrowing rose to £14.3B in February 2026 from £12.1B in the same month last year, exceeding expectations of £8.5B. 

Germany (DAX:IND) +1.23% to 23,111. Producer prices in Germany declined 3.3% Y/Y February.

France (CAC:IND) +0.86% to 7,869. 

In other parts of Europe, the manufacturing confidence indicator in Denmark rose to 94 in March.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) moved 0.70% higher to 587.7. The rebound was supported by declining oil prices and followed sharp losses in the previous session, when cautious messaging from central banks across the region dampened investor sentiment.

UK natural gas futures dropped to around 150 pence per therm, pulling back from a more than three-year high. Brent crude futures fell toward $108 per barrel on Friday after surging above $113 in the previous session.

Coming up in the session: Euro Area trade balance.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down 1 basis point to 4.27%. 

Germany's 10-year yield was down 1 basis point to 2.95%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 1 basis point to 4.85%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)
